'''Lankor VI''' was the sixth planet in its system, and was orbited by three moons. It had a temperate climate and was home to the [[Lankorian]]s, an intelligent species of skinny bipeds, with long arms and a sugar-loaf shaped head. The Lankorian civilization was feudal and not significantly industrialized by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. An important town on the planet was [[Rham'Dhaam]].<ref name="Le facteur X" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the planet was controlled by the [[Flight Unlimited Group]] (FUG), an Imperial-aligned corporation that specialized in designing high-speed spacecraft. They stationed a number of capital ships in orbit around the planet, including an [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor cruiser]], a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] and a [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|dreadnaught]]. The Lankorians were largely ambivalent about FUG control, although its presence did allow for embryonic star trade. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] managed to establish a sleeper cell of local on the planet, in preparation for eventual liberation.<ref name="Le facteur X" />

The FUG established a research base [[Meter/Legends|75 kilometers]] from Rham'Dhaam, within a large mountain. There, they developed a prototype starship called the ''[[Trait Luminique]]''. It possessed incredible energy boosters and a powerful [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. Word of this prototype reached the Rebel Alliance through Professor [[Adazian Loh'Khar]]. A dissident [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] working for the FUG, Loh'Khar worked for the FUG under duress; the FUG threatened his family living on his homeworld of [[Arthézia]]. However, when the Alliance freed his family, Loh'Khar fed them information on the ship. While most of the Alliance was preparing to attack the gathered Imperial forces at Endor, a small strike force was sent to rescue Loh'Khar and steal the prototype.<ref name="Le facteur X" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lankor VI appeared in an article in [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']], a French roleplaying magazine. The adventure revolved around a group of Rebel characters traveling [[Time travel/Legends|back in time]] to the period of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]].<ref name="Le facteur X" />
